summary: Iter 37 RETEST of CapitalDetailModal bulk-delete fix. Primary bug (page reload) STAYS FIXED — nav_count diff=0, URL unchanged across both bulk and single delete. However the secondary issue from iter 36 (modal closes/unmounts after successful bulk delete) is NOT FIXED. Despite the proposed fix moving setSelectionMode(false) before await Promise.allSettled, the Sermaye Detay modal still unmounts after bulk delete completes — total-capital-amount becomes invisible, dashboard is shown behind. The defensive Dialog onOpenChange={(open)=>{if(!open) onClose();}} doesn't help because Radix is itself firing onOpenChange(false) due to focus loss when the focused bulk-delete-btn unmounts during the toolbar swap.

ui_bugs.issue: Modal still closes after bulk delete completes despite setSelectionMode(false) being moved before await. RCA: When user clicks bulk-delete-btn, the click handler synchronously calls setSelectionMode(false) which causes the toolbar to re-render WITHOUT the bulk-delete-btn (it's swapped for the 'Seç' button). The currently-focused element (bulk-delete-btn) is removed from DOM mid-event. Radix Dialog's focus-trap detects focus leaving the dialog and calls onOpenChange(false) → onClose() → modal unmounts. The defensive onOpenChange guard (only calling onClose on open=false) does NOT help because Radix is the one firing it with open=false.

passed_tests: 'Seç' button (enter-selection-mode-btn) visible & clickable on cash tab
Selection mode toolbar shows toggle-select-all-btn, bulk-delete-btn, exit-selection-mode-btn
Selecting 2 rows updates Sil button label correctly to 'Sil (2)'
Bulk delete: URL unchanged, no framenavigated event → NO page reload (PRIMARY BUG STAYS FIXED)
Single-row delete also: no reload, no nav events
failed_tests: Modal stays open after bulk delete — STILL FAILS, modal unmounts
Selection mode auto-exits to 'Seç' button after bulk delete — N/A because modal already gone
Tab switch cash↔inventory regression — N/A (modal closed before this step could run)
action_items: Modal close after bulk delete is NOT fixed by current approach. Try ONE of these: (a) Add onInteractOutside={(e)=>e.preventDefault()} and/or onFocusOutside={(e)=>e.preventDefault()} handlers on <DialogContent> to prevent Radix from auto-closing on focus loss. This is the cleanest fix. (b) Before setSelectionMode(false), explicitly move focus to a stable element inside the dialog: e.g. dialogTitleRef.current?.focus() or document.querySelector('[data-testid="capital-tab-cash"]')?.focus(). (c) Wrap state updates in flushSync from react-dom and refocus next render. Recommended: Option (a) — add `onInteractOutside={(e)=>e.preventDefault()}` to DialogContent. This is the standard Radix workaround for this focus-trap quirk.
critical_code_review_comments: The current fix order: setBulkDeleting(true) → setSelectionMode(false) → await Promise.allSettled. React batches the two synchronous setStates so re-render happens immediately, removing the focused bulk-delete-btn from DOM. Radix Dialog FocusOutside detects this and dispatches onOpenChange(false). The defensive onOpenChange does nothing because Radix legitimately wants to close.

Better approach: leave selectionMode unchanged during the async delete; show 'Siliniyor...' on bulk-delete-btn (which already happens). Only after Promise.allSettled, set selectionMode=false. The button stays mounted and disabled during deletion → no focus loss. The original concern about focus-trap-during-disabled is unfounded; disabled buttons CAN keep focus in modern browsers.
Alternative: add onInteractOutside={(e)=>e.preventDefault()} to <DialogContent> to make modal only closeable via explicit Kapat / X / onClose call.
Reload+refreshCapital+fetchData triple call still happens after every delete; consider consolidating.

success_rate.frontend: 62% (8/13) — primary no-reload goal PASS, modal-stays-open RETEST FAIL

seed_data_creation: Attempted to seed 4 manual_deposit movements via POST /api/capital/adjust → all returned 422 (likely backend payload schema mismatch — uses different field names). Test fell back to deleting first 2 existing movements (₺3.338.900 → ₺3.338.830 capital change confirms backend deletion succeeded).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 37 retest result: bulk-delete no-reload is solid, but modal-stays-open is still broken with same RCA as iter 36. The 'move setSelectionMode before await' fix did not help because the focused button still unmounts synchronously. Recommend trying onInteractOutside/onFocusOutside preventDefault on DialogContent (Option a in action_items). After re-fix, run the same playwright flow: login → click capital-amount-display → enter-selection-mode-btn → click 2 rows → bulk-delete-btn → wait 3s → check total-capital-amount is still visible. Note: /api/capital/adjust returns 422 for {amount, type, description} payload — schema may differ; check backend if seeding needed.
rca_of_the_issue: Bulk delete handler synchronously calls setSelectionMode(false) which causes React to re-render, removing the bulk-delete-btn (currently focused due to user click) from DOM. Radix Dialog's FocusScope detects focus leaving its content and dispatches onOpenChange(false), which the parent's onClose handler honors → modal unmounts. The 'defensive' onOpenChange guard `if (!open) onClose()` doesn't prevent this because Radix is *legitimately* asking to close. The fix needs to either prevent Radix from auto-closing on focus-loss (onInteractOutside/onFocusOutside preventDefault) OR keep the focused element mounted (don't swap toolbar until after async completes).
